headline: World's Oldest Tortoise Caught in Viral Crypto Death Scam

title.text: Fake X account poses as his vet sparking global false reports of Jonathan's death while soliciting crypto donations
title.visual_subject: A photo of the Seychelles giant tortoise, Jonathan, on the grounds of the governor's mansion on Saint Helena

intro.script_text: The world's oldest living land animal, a Seychelles giant tortoise named Jonathan, has been targeted by a viral crypto death scam. An X account posing as his vet claimed he had passed away, sparking global false reports and soliciting cryptocurrency donations.
intro.visual_subject: A photo of the Seychelles giant tortoise, Jonathan, on the grounds of the governor's mansion on Saint Helena

key_points.script_text: The fake X account claimed Jonathan had passed away, but checks by the Guardian revealed the account was based in Brazil.
key_points.visual_subject: A screenshot of the fake X account claiming Jonathan's death

key_points.script_text: Jonathan has lived on the grounds of the governor's mansion on Saint Helena since 1882, and is a local celebrity who has appeared on the reverse of St Helena's 5p coin.
key_points.visual_subject: A photo of Jonathan next to a BBC news page on an iPad

key_points.script_text: Despite being blind from cataracts and having lost his sense of smell, Jonathan remains hale and hearty with a strong appetite for bananas.
key_points.visual_subject: A photo of Jonathan lying on the ground, slightly shrouded in shrubbery

summary.script_text: The viral crypto death scam targeting Jonathan has been exposed, and the tortoise is safe. The fake X account claiming his death sparked global false reports and soliciting cryptocurrency donations.
summary.visual_subject: A photo of Jonathan lying on the ground, slightly shrouded in shrubbery
